serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Special Flavoured Eggplant, often served as a side dish with rice, is approximately 250 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Eggplant, cooked, stir-fried
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central for 'Eggplant, cooked, stir-fried', representing a typical preparation with oil and seasonings. Specific 'flavoured' preparation may vary slightly.

gi_source.title: Eggplant, cooked
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated based on cooked eggplant (GI 15-20) from the University of Sydney GI Database, adjusted upwards to 50 to account for added sugars and oil in the 'flavoured' preparation. Considered low GI per convention.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce and other seasonings.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation due to its sodium content and consider requesting a low-sodium preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index of eggplant, but potential for added sugars in sauce.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish has a low glycemic index, making it a suitable choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided added sugars in the sauce are not excessive.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium content and fat from cooking oil.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular health, moderate consumption is advised due to sodium and fat content; opting for less oil and reduced sodium can improve its suitability.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Special Flavoured Eggplant (风味茄子, Fēngwèi qiézi) is a popular Chinese vegetarian dish featuring tender eggplant pieces stir-fried or braised in a rich, savory, and often slightly sweet and tangy sauce. The sauce typically includes ingredients like soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, garlic, and ginger, sometimes with a hint of chili. The eggplant absorbs the flavors beautifully, resulting in a soft, melt-in-your-mouth texture. Nutritionally, it is a good source of dietary fiber and can be relatively low in calories if prepared with less oil. However, sodium content can be moderate to high due to soy sauce. It is generally considered a low glycemic index dish, making it a suitable option for those managing blood sugar levels. This dish is widely enjoyed across China and in Chinese restaurants globally, including Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from a generic stir-fried eggplant entry in USDA FoodData Central, as an exact match for 'Special Flavoured Eggplant' was not found. Glycemic index is an analogous estimate based on cooked eggplant, adjusted for typical preparation.
notes: Trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are naturally zero as this is a plant-based dish. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated based on similar stir-fried vegetable dishes from USDA FoodData Central, as specific values were not available for the adapted source.
